sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The IO class, implemented in Cython, is a critical component of the AnalysisG framework, providing high-performance input/output operations primarily for ROOT files. It acts as an interface between the Python environment and an underlying C++ I/O engine, enabling efficient data reading, metadata management, and iteration over complex event structures. This class is designed to streamline the process of accessing and processing experimental data, often involving large datasets.

sect1.title: The IO Class
sect1.para: IO is a cdef class, signifying its implementation as a Cython extension type. This allows for direct and optimized interaction with C++ objects and types, which is essential for handling ROOT files efficiently. It encapsulates a pointer (self.ptr) to a C++ io object, managing its lifecycle and exposing its functionalities to Python.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. It initializes a new C++ io object and assigns its pointer to self.ptr. It also initializes self.data_ops to NULL and self.meta_data as an empty Python dictionary. It triggers a PCM (presumably "Progress Control Mechanism") using self.ptr.trigger_pcm().

__init__(self, root = []): The Python initializer. It takes an optional root argument, which can be a list of ROOT file paths or a single path string. It initializes self.prg (for tqdm progress bar) to None and sets self.Files property based on the root input.
root
A list of ROOT file paths or a single ROOT file path string.

__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It calls self.ptr.root_end() to finalize ROOT operations and then deallocates the C++ io object pointed to by self.ptr, ensuring proper memory management.

sect2.title: Special Methods (Iterator Protocol)
sect2.para: The IO class implements the Python iterator protocol, allowing it to be used directly in for loops.

listitem.para:
__len__(self): Returns the maximum number of entries across all ROOT files managed by the IO object. This is typically used by tqdm to determine the total progress.
The maximum size (number of entries) of the ROOT files.

__iter__(self): Initializes the iterator. If a progress bar (self.prg) is not already initialized, it creates a tqdm instance. It then calls self.ptr.root_begin() to prepare the C++ I/O engine for reading and retrieves the data operations pointer (self.data_ops). It also clears self.skip (a dictionary used to track processed files) and returns self.
The IO class instance, configured as an iterator.

__next__(self): Retrieves the next data item during iteration.
It iterates through the data_ops (a C++ map of file paths to data_t pointers).
For each file, it checks self.skip to see if it has been fully processed.
It uses switch_board(itr.second) to convert the C++ data_t object into a Python dictionary, merging it into output.
It updates self.skip for the current file based on itr.second.next(), which advances the C++ data pointer.
If no data is retrieved (i.e., output is empty), it means iteration is complete, so it calls self.ptr.root_end(), resets self.prg, and raises StopIteration.
It updates the tqdm progress bar and adds the filename to the output dictionary.
A dictionary containing the next data item from the ROOT files.
StopIteration
When all data from all configured ROOT files has been processed.

cpdef dict MetaData(self): Retrieves metadata associated with the ROOT files. It populates self.meta_data by iterating through self.ptr.meta_data (a C++ map of file paths to meta objects). For each meta object, it creates a Python Meta object and potentially enables PyAMI processing if self.EnablePyAMI is true.
A dictionary where keys are file paths (Python strings) and values are Meta objects containing metadata.
cpdef void ScanKeys(self): Triggers the underlying C++ I/O engine to scan the keys (trees, branches, leaves) present in the configured ROOT files. This populates the self.ptr.keys structure.
cpdef void begin(self): Explicitly calls self.ptr.root_begin() to initialize the ROOT I/O structure. This is typically handled by __iter__ but can be called manually.

cpdef void end(self): Explicitly calls self.ptr.root_end() to finalize the ROOT I/O structure and release resources. This is typically handled by __dealloc__ or __next__ (on StopIteration) but can be called manually.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties provide controlled access to the configuration and state of the IO object.

listitem.para:
@property Keys: Retrieves a nested dictionary representing the structure of keys (trees, branches, leaves) found in the ROOT files. It calls self.ptr.scan_keys() to ensure the keys are up-to-date.
A dictionary: filename -> tree_name -> branch_name -> [leaf_names].

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The IO class heavily relies on Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class IO: Declares IO as a Cython extension type.
cdef io *ptr: A C++ pointer to an io object, managed by the Cython class.
from libcpp cimport bool, map, pair, string, vector: Imports C++ standard library types.
from AnalysisG.core.io cimport io: Imports the C++ io class declaration from its .pxd file.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *, from AnalysisG.core.meta cimport Meta, meta, from AnalysisG.core.structs cimport data_t, data_enum, switch_board: Imports C++ declarations from corresponding .pxd files.
tqdm: Used for displaying progress bars in the console.
dereference as deref: Used to dereference C++ pointers.
env, enc, env_vec, enc_list: Utility functions (presumably from AnalysisG.core.tools) for converting between Python and C++ data types (strings, lists, vectors).
data_t*: Pointer to a C++ data_t structure, representing data from a ROOT tree entry.
meta*: Pointer to a C++ meta structure, representing metadata.
switch_board: A function (likely from AnalysisG.core.structs) to convert C++ data_t to a Python dictionary.
